message:
fix(service/timeout): prefer completed inner future at timeout boundary

TimeoutFuture previously checked the sleep deadline before polling the
inner future, meaning a response that arrived at exactly the timeout
instant was discarded in favor of an Elapsed error.  This caused
non-deterministic flakes in tests and production when the inner
completed on the same tick as the deadline.

Reorder to poll the inner future first: if it is Ready, return it
regardless of the deadline.  Only check elapsed when the inner is
Pending, matching the principle of least surprise (completed work should
never be thrown away).

Also removes the unused `started` field from TimeoutFuture, simplifying
the struct and its constructors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
